title: Leatt Presents: All Downhill From Here – Paul Kalifatidi
content:
Leatt drops a new apparel capsule for the Nukeproof Axess Racing Team. Inspired by rave culture of the 1980s, it’s got smiley faces, Day-Glo and a refreshing rejection of the seriousness prevalent in World Cup downhill racing.

The spirit of 1980s rave culture has landed on the unsuspecting world of Downhill Mountain Bike Racing. Leatt, a motocross and bike gear brand from Cape Town, South Africa, has taken inspiration from rave’s euphoric energy and transplanted it into a radical new outfit for the Nukeproof Axess Racing (NAR) team in the 2026 UCI World Cup Downhill Series.

Drawing on the exhilaration of the rave scene: an underground realm of warehouse parties, sweat-drenched basements, abandoned shops, and crumbling railway arches, Leatt’s vision fuses the uniform of Day-Glo, baggy silhouettes and smiley-face T-shirts with technical clothing to make the perfect hybrid of two eras.

More than just fashion, it’s pure love in the moment, unbridled emotion and a deliberate rejection of everything the status quo holds close.
The parallels are clear, even if the contrasts feel sharper than ever. Let’s go racing. And let’s make it loud.

 

See more at Leatt.

The post Leatt Presents: All Downhill From Here appeared first on The Radavist.
author: Paul Kalifatidi
